summary: Full E2E testing of Baba PG House marketing website. Backend API (5/5 tests passed). Frontend all major features verified via Playwright.

ui_bugs.issue: Missing data-testid attributes on hero CTA buttons (Call Now, Book a Visit, WhatsApp Us), contact form inputs/select/textarea/submit button, and floating WhatsApp button. Automation had to fall back to placeholder/text selectors.

integration_issues.flow: WhatsApp floating button - popup with both numbers
integration_issues.issue: Floating WhatsApp button is blocked by the Emergent badge overlay (pointer events intercepted), could not click it in testing. The two phone numbers ARE visible in the Contact section side info but the popup with both numbers from the floating button was not testable.

action_items: Add data-testid attributes to hero CTA buttons, contact form fields, submit button, and WhatsApp floating button for better testability
WhatsApp floating button is blocked by Emergent badge overlay in preview environment (not a real bug for production)
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is small and clean (~126 lines) - no split needed
MongoDB _id is NOT excluded from insert_one doc but since the doc does not include _id field it uses an auto-generated ObjectId which is not returned - OK
Email send failure is silently caught and logged - correct behavior per spec
All env vars properly loaded from .env - no hardcoded secrets
CORS allows * origins - acceptable for public marketing site

success_rate.frontend: 95% - all core features working, WhatsApp popup blocked by testing env overlay only

seed_data_creation: 2 test enquiries submitted via contact form API during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend tests pass. Contact form submits and shows success message. Hero section, nav, rooms pricing, FAQ accordion, footer, dark mode toggle, mobile layout all working. WhatsApp floating button popup could not be tested due to Emergent badge overlay intercepting clicks in the preview environment. Add data-testid attributes to interactive elements for better E2E coverage.
